methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
BurnBot is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Anukool Lakhina that has raised $25M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a peer set of 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Watershed, Graphyte, Fourth Power, Steady Energy) supplemented by 1 public comparable and 8 additional private references in the climate and energy space.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $25M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implying $50M. Peer-median analysis examines the 4 direct comparables, which span $110M to $1.8B, with a median valuation of $175M. This wider range reflects heterogeneity within the climate sector; Watershed's $1.8B valuation represents a clear outlier.

The triangulated analysis places BurnBot in a range of $42M (low) to $201M (high), with a median estimate of $112M. The low figure reflects conservative stage-progression logic; the high end approaches peer-median levels. The significant dispersion between methods—particularly the influence of Watershed's outlier valuation on the peer set—creates material uncertainty. BurnBot's specific competitive positioning, technology maturity, and capital efficiency relative to these comps remain key variables that would warrant closer examination before final pricing.
